title: verbascum rohlenae
intro: Verbascum rohlenae, also known as Rohlena's mullein, is a rare and endangered plant species within the mullein family. This biennial herb is primarily found in the mountainous regions of Central Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of Verbascum rohlenae contain anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including respiratory problems, wounds, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, offering potential health benefits such as soothing coughs, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing.
